channel.title: Comments for page "Stealing Solidarity Hub"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Stealing Solidarity Hub"

item.title: Whoa.
item.content:encoded:
I binged this entire Canon in one sitting not too long ago for Crencon and it was so over the top and so ridiculously anime that it fried my brain for an entire day.

Even trying to remember the entire thing fried my brain again…

Upvote +9001

item.title: Opening Titles
item.content:encoded: When I first found this hub, and read the short bio, this came into mind.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
For the YouTube playlist, you can sent a PM to Deci' about that.

And for mine, I have seen in the comments many suggestions of new songs. It's interesting, probably that I'll make a big addition of all these suggestions in a near future.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A bunch of the songs linked on Decibelle's playlist are broken. Also, recommending the addition of Bill and Brenda Sutton's Sense Of Wonder to the playlist.

item.title: UPDATE : Now, it's done !
item.content:encoded:
So, with permission of both Decibelles and Zyn, my playlist is now officially on the hub !

Many thanks to the two and, for all the guys who want to wrote for this canon and who want to listening inspirational songs, you can go and listen ! ^^

item.title: So, for the playlist...
item.content:encoded:
I've seen recently that there still wasn't a real playlist since the death of Grooveshark. So… I made my own, on Spotify.

It's here. I took most of the songs of the YouTube playlist and add some of my own. I wanted to add songs that are really punchy and/or fun and I think I made a good list.

But I don't know if I can put directly the link of this playlist on the hub. Can I ?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This canon is criminally underrated (though I realize that SCP-2085 has over 300 upvotes and the original series isn't doing half-bad either) and deserves to be written about more! Most of the other canons have so many rules and preconceptions you need to have, like a certain GoI or some end of the world scenario. Here, it's just "Black Rabbit Company (and maybe not even that), go nuts, and be happy!" Wholeheartedly +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I apologize for my rash commentary because my thoughts on the subject is oriented around my own preferences for a canon which shouldn’t be imposed on the whole site, which makes critique of a nature I have made pointless because there should be a canon for everyone. This one is just not for me. Sorry for any disrespect. I’ll definitely start looking for something I like instead of ruining your fun.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not sure about you but cyborg catgirls seem pretty spooky.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You ever notice how it’s always people who have never written a word on the wiki who think they’re speaking for anyone but themselves? You think this doesn’t belong on the site as though you own it or have any place from which to speak. Maybe contribute before you make demands of content. This is entitlement of the highest degree.

You special snowflakes just really can’t handle seeing non horror writing on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why should it be on its own wiki? It’s goofy and outlandish but no more so than 076 or 682. Moreover, the plot of the canon, the primary plot driver, and the protagonists and antagonists are all SCPs or parts of the Foundation. Moreover, the wiki is big enough to support the existence of such a piece - there is zero need to compromise and there always will be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted. I don't think SCP and catgirls are a good mix.

I'm not bigoted against people with these tastes it's just that when I get immersed in a canon it's primarily because of the premise, and I don't think that something outlandish such as catgirls and furries and memes are a good fit with the theme of the site. It also gives a bad look to new people who want to explore the site, who will see the pictures you have and the premise you're presenting and they will instantly cringe. Personally I don't think things like this belong on the site, it even says in the "How to write for this canon" page that it really doesn't have anything to do with the Foundation. So why isn't this on it's own wiki?

Don't burn me at the stake for stating my opinion, but there is a large contingent of people who think that this type of content should be on it's own wiki, whether it be a sister site or not. RCP authority is primarily composed of people with controversial opinions, but the community shouldn't be divided like it is. We should all come together for a compromise on what kind of tone the wiki pages should have.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: O5 Council is nanobot cancer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So in short this is SCP eyebleach with wack shenanigans… I love it

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I believe that in this context, the Overseers ARE the O5 Council.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What is an overseer in relation to this canon? It's not the o5 council, as far as I can tell, and I can't find any other references to the word besides the o5 council, so I am extremely confused on this front. Please help.

item.title: Major question that needs to be answered
item.content:encoded:
If there are five cyborg catgirls, does that mean that there are five cyborg dogboys chasing them?

Like for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction so obviously some madman decided to make five cyborg dogboys, like how Mojo Jojo and HIM made the Rowdy-Ruff Boys to counter the Power-Puff girls.

Right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Will Spotify work for the playlist?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, see above.

To elaborate, we don't remove tales just because they involve SCPs that no longer exist on the site.

This is still an amazing series and enjoyable on its own merits. You don't need to read the old SCP-2722 to understand it. It's still collaborative, as all canons are by definition by at least three authors.

And who knows, maybe someone right now is working on a new SCP-2722 that works even better with this canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are works set on this canon still viable? if not, shouldn't this be archived or stripped from the collaboration tag?

item.title: Re: SCP-2722
item.content:encoded: The canon will remain as the original author of 2722 has no claim to works derived from his works.

item.title: SCP-2722
item.content:encoded: So, now that 2722 has been deleted, what's going to happen to this canon? I just started reading it and it is awesome, but I hope nothing bad will happen to the story itself because of this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To say this is one of the best things written the site on the site would be a lie, because Stealing Serenity is easily the best thing ever written in the history of ever. It's so bombastic and fittingly counter to everything on the site in a way that's just fun to read. Because no matter how justified the Foundation is, they're still kinda dicks, and totally deserved this happening to them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Since the title refers to stealing the ship itself, it’d be one of the latter two.

I’m unsure of how translating stuff like group names like that work so I can’t speak to thaf.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello, I have a little question. I'm interested on translating this hub to my mother tongue (spanish), but the title could be translated in different ways depending on its meaning. I get that the title probably is intended to sound like it has several possible meanings, but said meanings are lost in traslation, I'm afraid.

I could discuss it with other translators, but I figured that it would be better to ask directly to whoever came up with the title.

I can translate it in three ways:

-Robando Solidaridad: Stealing solidarity (The abstract concept of solidarity, that is)

-Robando la Solidaridad: Stealing THE Solidarity (That would refer to the ship itself, with translated name)

-Robando la Solidarity: Stealing the "Solidarity" (Same as previous, but with untranslated name for the ship)

Edit: Also, Would you like me to leave the name "Black Rabbit Company" untranslated?

item.title: Re: Please Review.
item.content:encoded: Alright, thanks

item.title: Re: Please Review.
item.content:encoded: Make the SCP first, gauge the response to the article before writing the tale, and if the article does well enough to stay on the site, ask the audience if they would be interested in reading such a tale.

item.title: Please Review.
item.content:encoded: I'm planning on making an SCP, and I want to also make a Tale involving him and this Canon, any tips?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one has always been fun to read. I like it for the humor of the character's personalities. The SCP document for this is great as well. It's just a nice break from the normal, serious tone of most tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
2 reasons for instant up vote:
1)

Skillset: Administration, hand-to-hand combat, swordsmanship, tactical command, fixer, staff therapist, herding cats

2)

Humor: Balanced

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagine if the Guardians of the Galaxy were an anime that aired on Toonami during the late 90s.

It just occured to me that you just described Outlaw Star here. Which incidentally also had catgirls, Ha!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure what i read but i had fun reading it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Some div blocks here and there, maybe with overflow capabilities so you can save some space. A space theme background would be nice IMO. Just spit-balling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, if you've got suggestions / methods, feel free to bring them up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Honestly, I think this hub can be cleaned up quite a bit with some aesthetic touches. It's functional as is though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have added a Grooveshark player for Decibelle's playlist (thanks Armstrong), and artwork for Wizard (thanks Jenssosaurus!)

The hub grows strong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So, i took Decibelle's playlist and i added some things, and i condensed it into an HTML player from Grooveshark. It should be playable from the page if you copy and paste this code.

 [[html]] <center> <object width="500" height="250" classid="clsid:D27CDB6E-AE6D-11cf-96B8-444553540000" id="gsPlaylist10404664818" name="gsPlaylist10404664818"><param name="movie" value="http://grooveshark.com/widget.swf" /><param name="wmode" value="window" /><param name="allowScriptAccess" value="always" /><param name="flashvars" value="hostname=grooveshark.com&playlistID=104046648&p=0&bbg=000000&bth=000000&pfg=000000&lfg=000000&bt=ffffff&pbg=ffffff&pfgh=ffffff&si=ffffff&lbg=ffffff&lfgh=ffffff&sb=ffffff&bfg=a11212&pbgh=a11212&lbgh=a11212&sbh=a11212" /><object type="application/x-shockwave-flash" data="http://grooveshark.com/widget.swf" width="500" height="250"><param name="wmode" value="window" /><param name="allowScriptAccess" value="always" /><param name="flashvars" value="hostname=grooveshark.com&playlistID=104046648&p=0&bbg=000000&bth=000000&pfg=000000&lfg=000000&bt=ffffff&pbg=ffffff&pfgh=ffffff&si=ffffff&lbg=ffffff&lfgh=ffffff&sb=ffffff&bfg=a11212&pbgh=a11212&lbgh=a11212&sbh=a11212" /><span><a href="http://grooveshark.com/search/playlist?q=Stealing%20Solidarity%20Jo%C3%A3o" title="Stealing Solidarity by João on Grooveshark">Stealing Solidarity by João on Grooveshark</a></span></object></object> </center> [[/html]]

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ones that are permanently linked to the black rabbit company for me:
Most of these ran through my head at some point while reading this first arc.
  • Glitch Mob's albums:
    • Animus Vox - "Drive it like you stole it" especially. "Fortune Days" is what was playing in my head after the last part of the epilogue where the credits would roll in a movie.
    • Love Death Immortality: "Skullclub" is my nomination for the Black Rabbit Company's themesong. "I Need My Memory Back" was Wizard in Phase 3, "Can't Kill Us", "Skytoucher" and "Carry the Sun" were my mental background songs for most of Phase 4. ("Carry the Sun" starts at the line “Universal boundary passed. We have exited baseline.” 1:00 is “Then I’m with you.” 2:00 is "100%". 3:10 is "101%". 4:35 is "Boss grinned at wizard, gave him a nod.")
  • Destroid - The Invasion : "Bounce" is 100% Nanami's concerto in Phase 4 ("It is too heavy!" X3). "Flip the Switch", "Activation", and "Put It Down" are best listened to as a set in that order, and make me think of their creation/early missions. Every song on this album is just begging to have a tale in this canon based off of it, really.
  • The Heavy - "Short Change Hero"
  • Tom Petty and the Heartbreakers - "I won't back down"
  • Zomboy - "Pirate Hooker", "Game Time", "City 2 City", "Raptor"
  • Feint - "Fury"
  • Pegboard Nerds - Hero. The original mix is epic, but the Teminite Remix is an even better fit
  • Varien - "Gunmetal Black"
  • Anything by Nero

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You da bessssst.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Someone called?

Linked to Djoric in chat, it should be up in the hub :3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If someone were to hypothetically assemble the playlist and hypothetically post it to this hub, that'd be pretty neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think I found a new favorite thing on this site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Best series since the Cool War. :D

item.title: Ha-ha! Success!
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to Armstrong for unborking all this.

There'll be more art from Jenssosaurus, don't you worry.

And for the rest, go right on ahead. The canon is open for you to play in. Draw art! Write tales! Do interpretive musical numbers! Whatever suits your fancy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's mostly mine, I'm afraid. But I have no idea how to fix it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me on mobile the catgirls are walking out of the picture border and covering the sidebar. I actually kind of like it. Does wonders showing their "I don't give a fuck" attitude

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am working on getting all the pictures fixed. Please be patient. Can't say anything about phones though. Mobile compatibility is lowest priority.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Picture's broken for me too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Picture seems broken from my phone. Otherwise, good hub, neat picture.
